Phase-30.NAME: KPprotoN Runtime Bridge
Phase-30.Goal: Connect KrotPN MTProto assignments to a runtime policy bridge that can activate issued SNI domains safely.

NonGoals.item-2: No user dashboard UI work yet.

Steps.step-1: Define the bridge interface for apply-domain-policy, runtime health, startup replay, and reconciliation.
Steps.step-2: Choose integration shape for KPprotoN/mtproto_proxy runtime with pinned dependency or isolated service boundary.
Steps.step-3: Implement startup replay from M-042 assignments into runtime policy state with idempotent retries.
Steps.step-4: Implement degraded-mode semantics when the runtime bridge is unavailable during verified onboarding.
Steps.step-5: Wire a scheduler reconciliation task without impacting existing anti-abuse scheduler jobs.
Steps.step-6: Add tests for policy apply success, bridge unavailable, replay after restart, and no-secret logging.

Verification.command: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_scheduler.py -v

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Active assignments replay into policy state after backend or bridge restart.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Bridge failure does not break normal KrotPN login or VPN config access.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Verified users see an explicit pending/degraded proxy state if runtime activation is delayed.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Runtime logs include stable markers but no full proxy link, token, or secret.

Risks.risk-1: KPprotoN Erlang dependency drift can make runtime behavior non-reproducible unless pinned.
Risks.risk-2: Policy state can drift from Postgres unless reconciliation is designed early.
